</imports>

<findings>

[observation] [info] High cohesion (0.99) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] Given multiple external gateways, add shared resilience middleware (timeouts/retries) so each adapter doesn’t reimplement error handling.
[observation] [info] Server code cleanly isolates gateway adapters from schemas (coupling 0.01) which keeps backend changes from rippling into the viewer.
[observation] [info] `data-files.ts` acts as both schema and entrypoint; separating runtime wiring from type exports would make the package easier to tree-shake.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (0.99) — files are tightly interconnected
- Data-file schema entrypoint doubles as runtime entrypoint; split validation into its own package to slim the server bundle.
- Multiple gateway adapters (domain, rex, MCP) imply varied upstream APIs—codify common retry/logging policies to keep behavior uniform.
- Coupling stays near zero, so continue enforcing dependency rules that prevent viewer components or schema code from bleeding inward.
- Server code cleanly isolates gateway adapters from schemas (coupling 0.01) which keeps backend changes from rippling into the viewer.
- `data-files.ts` acts as both schema and entrypoint; separating runtime wiring from type exports would make the package easier to tree-shake.
- Given multiple external gateways, add shared resilience middleware (timeouts/retries) so each adapter doesn’t reimplement error handling.
- [call graph] 1042 internal calls, 21 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 0.98, coupling: 0.02)

</insights>
